title: Immunocytochemistry, Confocal Imaging, and Quantitative Analysis of iNeurons
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"61myi","text":"This protocol describes fixation, immunocytochemical staining, and spinning disk confocal imaging of induced neurons (iNeurons) at defined stages of differentiation (see dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.br9em93e for a typical differentiation protocol). The workflow includes antibody labeling of neuronal and synaptic markers followed by imaging using a Yokogawa CSU-W1 spinning disk confocal microscope. Downstream quantitative analysis pipelines are provided for the identification of tyrosine hydroxylase (TH)–positive neurons and for the quantification of synaptic protein puncta using automated segmentation and fluorescence analysis in Python.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7ryrp","text":"- Maintain consistent laser intensity and exposure times across all samples to allow quantitative comparisons.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":22,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"tez1y","text":"- All washes should be performed gently to avoid detaching neurons.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2btn9","text":"- Protect fluorescent samples from light whenever possible.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"uvdo8","text":"- Prepare all buffers fresh or verify that stored buffers are free of contamination.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1wqit","text":"- Image acquisition parameters should remain constant within experiments intended for quantitative analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"imw4q","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sxfhw","text":"**Image Acquisition Guidelines**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdu1q","text":"- Maintain identical exposure times and laser intensities for all samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ubis5","text":"- Acquire images using sequential channel acquisition to avoid spectral datasets.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare primary and secondary antibody dilutions in 3% BSA-PBS**.
Ensure that the confocal microscope and lasers are properly calibrated.
Remove culture medium from differentiated iNeurons.
Remove PFA and rinse cells once with PBS.
Incubate cells in 0.5% Triton X-100 in PBS for 15 minutes at room temperature.
Wash cells three times with PBST (0.02% Tween-20 in PBS).
Incubate cells in 3% BSA in PBS for 10 minutes at room temperature.
Wash cells three times with PBST.
Incubate for 3 hours at room temperature.
Wash cells three times with PBST.
Incubate cells with secondary antibodies for 1 hour at room temperature.
Wash cells three times with PBST.
Cells are now ready for imaging.
Images are acquired at room temperature using a Yokogawa CSU-W1 spinning disk confocal system mounted on a Nikon Eclipse Ti2-E microscope.
Lasers are applied sequentially using a Nikon LUN-F XL laser combiner.
Camera: Hamamatsu ORCA-Fusion BT CMOS (16-bit acquisition).
Time-lapse calcium imaging datasets are analyzed using a custom Python pipeline_.
ND2 stacks are read using the nd2 Python package_.
Segmentation is performed using CellposeSAM with a pretrained model optimized for neuronal soma.
Segmentation masks are filtered by area (100–10,000 pixels).
Mean fluorescence intensity is calculated across all frames.
CSV files containing centroid coordinates and fluorescence traces
Synaptic puncta from markers such as Synaptophysin (SYP) and Bassoon (BSN) are quantified using a custom Python pipeline_.
Maximum-intensity projection images are parsed for experimental metadata.
Background subtraction and segmentation detect synaptic puncta.
Object morphology and fluorescence intensity are measured.
Object-level colocalization analysis is performed between SYP and Bassoon puncta.
CSV files containing per-object and per-image measurements
Downstream statistical analysis and visualization are performed in R_.
Synaptic puncta labeled by SYP and Bassoon
Increase antibody concentration or incubation time
Increase wash duration or PBST concentration
Adjust acquisition settings or preprocessing parameters
